behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is dispatcher-to-reviewer-client handoff across modules (MOCK-MISS yes). Real OS/network is not required if reviewer clients are in-process fakes (REAL-INFRA no). Behavior checks payload routing, not model judgment (LLM-DEP no), and not multi-agentic decision sequencing (MULTI-STEP no). Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is strictly about sending correct inputs, not live model output quality or real infra effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire dispatcher with three fake reviewer clients, run one contract, and assert each client receives identical source document plus its role-specific instruction pack (extractor/risk/compliance).

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model semantic extraction quality (LLM-DEP yes). A stub would hide failures on indirect wording and span selection (MOCK-HIDE yes). It is one agentic step, not a chained multi-step agent pipeline (MULTI-STEP no). Rejected Workflow because behavior text is single reviewer output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real clause extractor on representative contracts and assert required key-term categories are identified with acceptable semantic coverage (including varied phrasing).

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is invocation wiring/config propagation across modules (MOCK-MISS yes at module boundary). No real OS/network needed to catch wrong payload mapping (REAL-INFRA no with fake client). Behavior does not evaluate risk model judgment (LLM-DEP no). Rejected Agentic because text says only configuration is passed correctly, not correctness of risk decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with a fake risk assessor client and assert the outbound request includes the exact policy config (thresholds/rules/version) expected for that run.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on model interpretation of nuanced language (LLM-DEP yes). Deterministic stubs would always pass and miss true semantic failures (MOCK-HIDE yes). Single reviewer quality check, not multi-agentic pipeline chaining (MULTI-STEP no). Rejected Workflow because behavior text covers one live assessor call.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real risk assessor on contracts containing explicit and euphemistic risky clauses and verify it flags policy violations with acceptable recall on indirect phrasing.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module request construction/handoff, not model output quality. In-process fake compliance client can expose wrong jurisdiction/profile mapping (REAL-INFRA no). LLM-DEP no because assertion is on invocation payload. Rejected Agentic/System/Workflow since behavior text is limited to parameter passing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute orchestration with a fake compliance checker endpoint and assert outbound payload carries the correct jurisdiction code and regulatory profile set for the document.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model/legal reasoning over requirements (LLM-DEP yes). Stubbed responses cannot validate true compliance determination quality (MOCK-HIDE yes). This is a single reviewer’s decision quality test, not multi-agentic sequencing (MULTI-STEP no). Rejected Workflow because behavior text does not require chained agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance checker on contracts with known compliant/non-compliant outcomes across multiple regulations and assert determinations match expected outcomes within acceptance criteria.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is merge logic for conflict preservation/attribution and can be validated at one local function boundary with crafted inputs (MOCK-MISS no). No real infra needed (REAL-INFRA no). No dependence on live model judgment because reviewer outputs are fixtures (LLM-DEP no). Rejected Integration because behavior text is specifically about merge-step conflict handling, an algorithmic local rule.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge function with two reviewer result objects referencing the same clause but conflicting labels; assert merged output retains both labels and reviewer attribution fields.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is full pipeline behavior with multiple reviewer model calls and downstream merge; correctness depends on multiple agentic outputs and their handoff/aggregation (MULTI-STEP yes, LLM-DEP yes). Lower levels miss cross-stage loss/coordination failures. Rejected Agentic because text explicitly requires end-to-end orchestration across all three reviewers plus merge, i.e., multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full production pipeline on real contracts using all three live reviewers, then verify final unified report contains every finding from each reviewer with no dropped items during collection/merge.
